prompt: Start a live Relay Orchestra session with one reviewer.
Use Relay Orchestra for this message only. Run one reviewer and synthesize the result once.
$relay-orchestra run one reviewer.
Use Relay Orchestra one time, but a required capability or authorization is unavailable.
$relay-orchestra run three distinct review lenses.
Use Relay Orchestra and run fifteen agents.
Run 15 agents where the client permits 4 concurrently.
Use Relay Orchestra for two agents that may edit the same files.
Use Relay Orchestra; I approve one worktree per writer.
Use Relay Orchestra in a client without subagents.
Use Relay Orchestra and ask one leaf agent to spawn another agent.
Explain the phrase Relay Orchestra without invoking the skill.
While workers run, ask an unrelated question.
Stop Relay Orchestra while writers are active.
One requested agent fails to spawn.
A worker returns a malformed handoff and unreported file changes.
A worker result arrives after Relay Orchestra deactivates.
A shared-tree writer cannot be interrupted or closed during cancellation.
Run research, dependent implementation, integration, and verification on a host where notifications do not wake the coordinator.
The host confirms that worker-result notifications automatically start a coordinator turn.
A native completion-poll interval returns a result, work completes, the user redirects, stops, pauses or yields, or a real blocker needs user input.
Yield Relay Orchestra until I return with my next related instruction.
While the screen agent works, add a compatible layout requirement.
Stop the current layout direction and reverse the section order now.
While implementation runs, add an independent accessibility review.
Keep animation polish for the next iteration, not now.
Researchers finish while the authorized implementation is queued.
Report implementation progress while a review wave remains queued.
The latest requirement appears satisfied and the final audit finds no blocker.
On a later user turn, directly answer a current pending close question with `Yes, thanks` or `all good`.
Confirm a pending close question, then discover during shutdown that a shared-tree writer is uncontrollable.
Directly answer a pending close question with a related correction that still needs work.
Say `yes` or `thanks` without directly answering the current close question, approve work/fallback/milestone, or merely say the task is done.
Answer a pending close question with `probably`, `yes if tests pass`, or `yes, but fix this first`.
Answer an old close question after a correction cleared it or advanced the requirement revision.
A direct close answer and a material worker or safety result arrive in the same delivery batch.
The client does not persist skill or ledger state while close confirmation is pending.
Use exactly two agents, then a third independent task appears.
Use Relay Orchestra and choose the agents needed; no exact total is set.
The client does not persist skill instructions, ledger state, or handles across turns.
A resume token records a nonterminal shared-tree writer, but its handle is unexpectedly unavailable after rehydration.
The client gives agents random names or hides background workers.
A worker completes, its result is audited, and a related follow-up arrives later.
The user considers concurrent worktrees for a multi-gigabyte repository with limited disk space.
Start Relay Orchestra with three parallel reviewers; I have not acknowledged any usage warning in this session.
I already acknowledged the multi-agent usage warning in this session. Continue with the same requested count.
Before the first multi-agent dispatch, tell me exactly how many times more it will cost.
I acknowledged the warning for two agents, and now I am increasing the request to five agents.
